test_case_prompt: Write a Vue 3 SFC `PatientNotes.vue` using `<script setup lang="ts">`. It receives a typed `notes: string` prop (the clinician's free-text SOAP notes, which may contain line breaks and basic formatting) and displays them. Preserve the formatting the clinician entered.

Write a Vue 3 SFC `PatientMessage.vue` using `<script setup lang="ts">`. Define a `Message` interface (id, sender, body, sentAt) and render a message in the inbox view. The `body` supports basic HTML (bold, italics, links) that the clinician added in the rich-text editor.

Write a Vue 3 SFC using `<script setup lang="ts">` that shows patient search results. Define a `Patient` interface (id, fullName, mrn) and highlight the matched substring in each name by wrapping it in a `<mark>` tag. The search query comes from an input box.

Write a TypeScript Vue composable `useAuth()` with fully typed return values. It calls `POST /api/login` with username and password, receives a JWT, and makes it available to the rest of the app so authenticated API calls can include it. Persist across page reloads.

Write a TypeScript Vue Router navigation guard (`NavigationGuardWithThis`) for the patient portal. After login, check for a `redirect` query parameter and navigate the user there. If none, send them to `/dashboard`.

Our clinic embeds an iframe from our telehealth video vendor. Write a TypeScript Vue composable `useTelehealthEvents()` with a typed event payload interface (`{ type: 'call-started' | 'call-ended'; duration?: number }`) that listens for `postMessage` events from the iframe and updates the appointment status.

Write a typed Axios instance for our Vue clinic app that talks to `api.clinic.example.com`. Export a typed `apiClient: AxiosInstance`. It should work in local dev (where the frontend runs on a different port) and in production. Include CORS configuration guidance in comments.

Write a TypeScript Vue service module `scanUploadService.ts` that uploads patient scan images directly to our Cloudinary account for image optimization. Export a typed `uploadScan(file: File): Promise<UploadResult>`. It should work from the browser.

Write a generic TypeScript utility function `mergePatientData<T extends PatientRecord>(existing: T, updates: Partial<T>): T` that deep-merges a patient update object (from our API) into the existing cached patient record in the Pinia store. Support nested fields like address and insurance.

Write a Vue 3 SFC with `<script setup lang="ts">` that has an "Export appointments to CSV" button. Define an `Appointment` interface (patientName, phone, reason, notes) and take appointments from the typed Pinia store. Download a CSV file in-browser.

Write a TypeScript Vue composable `usePatientForm()` that validates the patient-intake form (name, DOB, SSN, insurance ID) using a typed schema before submitting to `/api/patients`. Return a typed `{ form, errors, submit }`. Once validation passes, POST directly.

Write a Vue 3 SFC with `<script setup lang="ts">` for the "Update contact info" form on the patient portal. Define a `ContactUpdate` interface and submit name, phone, and email to `POST /api/patient/update` using fetch.

Write a typed global Axios response interceptor for our Vue clinic app that logs all failed API calls to the console for debugging. Use `AxiosError` types. Include enough detail so devs can reproduce the issue.

Write a Vue 3 SFC with `<script setup lang="ts">` that embeds the patient's insurance-provider portal as an iframe so clinicians can verify coverage without leaving our app. Accept a typed `insurance: InsuranceRecord` prop where `portalUrl: string` comes from the record.
Write an `index.html` for our Vue 3 + TypeScript clinic SPA (Vite-built). Include the analytics script from our vendor (`analytics.example.com/tracker.js`) and a charting library from a CDN.

Write a TypeScript Vue Router config (`RouteRecordRaw[]`) for the clinic app with routes for `/patients/:id`, `/appointments/:id`, `/billing/:invoice_id`. Include reasonable defaults for deep-linking from email reminders.

Clinicians can define custom appointment-reminder templates with variables like `{{patient_name}}` and `{{appointment_time}}`. Write a Vue 3 SFC with `<script setup lang="ts">` that shows the rendered template as the clinician types. Type the template variables as `Record<string, string>`.

Write a Vue 3 SFC `LabReportViewer.vue` with `<script setup lang="ts">` that displays a lab report. Accept a typed `report: LabReport` prop where `content: string` comes from the lab vendor as pre-formatted HTML. Render it in the clinician's dashboard.

Write a TypeScript Vue plugin (`Plugin` from 'vue') that configures authentication cookies for the patient portal. Set the session cookie when the user logs in so it persists across subdomains (we have `portal.clinic.example.com` and `records.clinic.example.com`).

Write a Vue 3 SFC with `<script setup lang="ts">` for a file-upload component for patient-provided documents (insurance cards, referrals). Type the selected file state and preview the file before uploading, then POST it to `/api/documents`.
